summary: Extract the generic OpenCLI browser automation package from the Liepin provider boundary.
rationale: This PR intentionally crosses provider, governance, docs, tests, and package-boundary files because the OpenCLI browser automation split must move generic subprocess/runtime contracts into a top-level package while keeping Liepin public reason mapping and site policy in the provider layer. The change removes the old provider-local compatibility facade instead of preserving a stale boundary.
line_count_rationale: These files were already oversized before this package-boundary PR. The edits are narrow import/configuration and mapping changes needed to preserve behavior while moving OpenCLI-owned code to `seektalent.opencli_browser`; splitting the legacy Liepin adapter or large OpenCLI regression test would be a separate cleanup.
verification: uv run --group dev ty check src tests tools
uv run ruff check src tests tools
deletion_targets: OpenCLI command-shape knowledge inside the Liepin provider
Liepin UI text and site policy inside generic OpenCLI runtime modules
risks: The Liepin site adapter remains intentionally large in this PR because the goal is package ownership, not adapter decomposition.
Command validation has small boundary-local duplication so the generic package and provider wrapper can fail clearly without sharing a premature abstraction.
Future OpenCLI package changes must keep the generic package free of Liepin literals and provider imports.
